title: inula crithmoides subsp. longifolia
intro: Inula crithmoides subsp. longifolia, also known as sea aster, is a plant species that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Inula crithmoides subsp. longifolia include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, sea aster is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
